title: Model Two-Dimensional Data with a Table
summary: Build and verify table structure in cooling-center capacity report through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain table structure from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply table structure without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Cooling-center capacity report case 1: tables are an efficient general-purpose tool for aligning page layout.
Cooling-center capacity report case 1: use a table only for two-dimensional data, give it a caption, and preserve row and column meaning without CSS.
Cooling-center capacity report case 1: put every cell directly inside table because browsers infer the missing rows.

steps.title: 05 · Read the cooling-center capacity report case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim preserves table structure when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: cooling-center capacity report.
steps.why: Model Two-Dimensional Data with a Table makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in cooling-center capacity report.

content.text: A data table represents relationships across rows and columns. Caption names the table’s purpose. Thead, tbody, and tfoot group meaningful row regions. Tr contains a row; th identifies a header; td contains data. Tables are not page-layout grids, and visual borders do not create data relationships. Apply it to cooling-center capacity report.
